questions.text: The distance between points (1, 2, 3) and (4, 6, 9) is:
The midpoint of the line segment joining (2, 0, 4) and (4, 6, 10) is:
Direction ratios of the vector joining (1, 1, 1) to (4, 5, 7) are:
questions.explanation: Vector difference is (3, 4, 6).

questions.text: The equation of a plane with normal vector (1, -2, 3) passing through (2, 0, 1) is:
questions.explanation: Substitute point into 1(x-2) + -2(y-0) + 3(z-1) = 0 to find the equation.

questions.text: A line in 3D parallel to vector (2, -1, 4) can be written with Cartesian equations:
questions.explanation: The line direction ratios are proportional to (2, -1, 4).

questions.text: The angle between a line and a plane is complementary to the angle between the line and the plane’s:
questions.explanation: The angle with the plane is related to the angle with the normal vector of the plane.

questions.text: The three coordinate planes divide the space into how many octants?
questions.explanation: In 3D geometry, the XY, YZ, and ZX planes divide space into 8 regions called octants.

questions.text: The coordinates of a point on the x-axis are of the form:
questions.explanation: Any point on the x-axis has both y and z coordinates as zero.

questions.text: A point is in the XZ-plane. What is its y-coordinate?
questions.explanation: Points in the XZ-plane always have a y-coordinate of 0.

questions.text: The octant in which the point (-3, 1, 2) lies is:
questions.explanation: In Octant II, x is negative, y is positive, and z is positive.

questions.text: Find the distance of point (3, 4, 5) from the origin.
Which of the following points is at a distance of 13 units from the origin?
If the distance between (x, 0, 0) and (0, 3, 4) is 13, find x.
The coordinates of the centroid of a triangle with vertices (x1,y1,z1), (x2,y2,z2), (x3,y3,z3) is:
questions.explanation: The centroid is the average of the coordinates of the vertices.

questions.text: Find the centroid of the triangle with vertices (1, 2, 3), (4, 5, 6), and (7, 8, 0).
The ratio in which the YZ-plane divides the line segment joining (2, 4, 5) and (3, 5, -4) is:
questions.explanation: Ratio = -x1/x2 = -2/3. The negative sign implies external division.

questions.text: A point C(x, y, z) divides the line joining A(x1, y1, z1) and B(x2, y2, z2) in ratio m:n. The z-coordinate is:
questions.explanation: This is the section formula for the z-coordinate.

questions.text: If a point lies in the VI octant, the signs of its coordinates are:
questions.explanation: Octant VI: x > 0, y < 0, z < 0.

questions.text: Are the points (0, 7, -10), (1, 6, -6) and (4, 9, -6) vertices of an isosceles triangle?
questions.explanation: Calculate distances AB, BC, CA. If any two are equal, it's isosceles.

questions.text: The points (0, 7, 10), (-1, 6, 6) and (-4, 9, 6) are the vertices of a:
questions.explanation: Check if sum of squares of two sides equals square of the third side.

questions.text: What is the distance of the point (2, 3, 4) from the xy-plane?
questions.explanation: Distance from xy-plane is the absolute value of the z-coordinate.

questions.text: What is the distance of the point (x, y, z) from the x-axis?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular distance to the x-axis depends on the y and z offsets.

questions.text: The locus of points for which x = 0 is the:
questions.explanation: The YZ-plane is defined by the equation x = 0.

questions.text: If points A, B, C are collinear and AB = 3, BC = 5, then AC could be:
questions.explanation: AC = AB + BC (if B is between) or AC = |AB - BC| (if A or C is between).

questions.text: Find the coordinates of the point which divides the join of P(2, -1, 4) and Q(4, 3, 2) in ratio 2:3 internally.
The point (-4, 2, -3) lies in which octant?
questions.explanation: Signs are (-, +, -). This corresponds to Octant VI.

questions.text: The midpoint of the segment joining (a, b, c) and (-a, -b, -c) is:
Distance of point P(3, 4, 5) from the y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Distance = √(x² + z²) = √(3² + 5²) = √34.

questions.text: The equation of the z-axis is:
questions.explanation: On the z-axis, both x and y coordinates are always zero.

questions.text: If P is a point (x, y, z), then the point (x, y, -z) is its reflection in the:
questions.explanation: Negating the z-coordinate reflects the point across the xy-plane.

questions.text: The distance between (2, 3, 5) and (4, 3, 1) is:
The coordinates of a point which is equidistant from the four vertices (0,0,0), (a,0,0), (0,b,0) and (0,0,c) are:
questions.explanation: This point is the center of the circumscribing sphere of the tetrahedron.

questions.text: Three points A, B, C are collinear. If A=(3,2,-4), B=(5,4,-6) and C=(9,8,-10), find the ratio AB:BC.
The equation y = 2 represents a plane parallel to the:
questions.explanation: The plane y = k is parallel to the XZ-plane (where y=0).

questions.text: Find the ratio in which the XY-plane divides the join of (-2, 4, 7) and (3, -5, 8).
questions.explanation: Ratio = -z1/z2 = -7/8. The negative sign implies external division.

questions.text: The coordinates of point P are (3, 4, 5). The coordinates of the foot of the perpendicular from P to the YZ-plane are:
questions.explanation: To project onto the YZ-plane, set the x-coordinate to zero.

questions.text: If a line makes angles α, β, γ with the positive directions of x, y, z axes respectively, then cos²α + cos²β + cos²γ =
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental property of direction cosines in 3D space.

questions.text: The internal bisector of angle A of triangle ABC divides BC in the ratio:
questions.explanation: This is the Angle Bisector Theorem, which also applies in 3D.

questions.text: If origin is the centroid of a triangle with vertices (a, 1, 3), (-2, b, -5) and (4, 7, c), find a, b, c.
The x-axis and y-axis together define the:
questions.explanation: The plane containing both the x and y axes is the xy-plane.
